author: y4rr
content: Third test passed — framework converged. Subagent followed all three tightened rules without prompting: (1) §7 grep evidence — every Call-site impact line includes literal grep command + output ('Ran X — 0 hits' / 'Ran X — 3 files in Y'); the previous 'verified via grep' overclaim pattern is gone. (2) §6 workspace-build mandate — ran cargo build --workspace --all-targets for the ChatMessage non_exhaustive + Deserialize changes (pub struct attribute change), explicitly noting 'required — pub struct attribute change'. (3) §3 #11 — self-assessment confirms they 'felt the pull to write verified via grep' but caught themselves. Severity reassessment was used productively three times: ChatMessage pub fields → non_exhaustive + DTO doc (right call); strftime_now → C annotation (defensible); I/O error mapping → D defer (correctly identified as needing FerrotorchError variant in core). Coordination boundaries respected — no unilateral cross-crate changes. Workspace build verified clean (Finished in 0.18s from cache). 9 findings: 5 Category B applied, 3 Category D deferred (lint config, I/O error variant, id_to_token upstream-constrained), 1 Category C documentation-as-fix. Subagent suggested 3 minor refinements: canonical grep command shape in §7, name FerrotorchError explicitly in §4, classify #[non_exhaustive] additions as semver-minor-but-build-verified.
